programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to the scene when there is an attempt on the life of FBI agent TC Fornell, with the assailant identified as Navy seaman Tyler Brown. The pair team up and visit the bar where Brown worked and bump into their mutual ex-wife Diane Sterling, who was operating undercover as an employee suspected of stealing customers' identities. Joe Spano and Melinda McGraw guest star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Returning from a six-month posting in the Gulf, Navy lieutenant commander Megan Huffner opens her front door to find her husband Noel dead in his chair. Gibbs and the team investigate and find the victim died three or four weeks earlier, and an unusual $100 bill in his wallet proves to be a vital clue. Meanwhile, Tony's father visits for Christmas and looks to rebuild their fractured relationship. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the scene of a fatal car crash involving marine lance corporal Chad Dunn and student Lyla Cutwright. Duckie notices that the woman's injuries do not match the specifics of the accident and there are no skidmarks found at the scene, leading Gibbs to declare they possibly have a murder and a suicide on their hands. Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy captain wearing a colourful costume underneath his uniform is shot and killed in a flower shop, and further investigation reveals he was leading a double life as a masked vigilante. Vance brings in Tony's ex-fiancee, journalist Wendy Miller, who claims the victim had been trying to expose a scandal, and as the team pursues the case, a real-estate scam is uncovered. Guest starring Perrey Reeves (Entourage)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy reservist is found dead in his home with marks around his neck, and Gibbs and the team discover he was working on a highly classified case for the department of defence. The circumstances surrounding the man's demise lead the investigators to suspect someone was secretly trying to drive him insane, bringing them into contact with Samantha Ryan, the director of a division specialising in psychological warfare. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Chief petty officer Leland Wiley dies of a heart attack just as he is about to reveal information on elusive arms dealer Agah Bayar. The team discovers the cardiac arrest was caused by someone hacking into a computer monitoring Wiley's pacemaker and increasing his heart rate to 400bpm. Meanwhile, a probationary agent tries to impress Gibbs and win a place on his team. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with Tamer Hassan and Matt L Jones

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: CSI: Miami

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Heaton's zero tolerance policy on drugs causes tensions at all levels within his staff, but produces an unexpected lead when a suspected baron's house is searched. Dan and Will interview a boxer they know from the club about a case of vandalism and hatch a plot to keep him out of trouble. Terry has his hands full minding a potentially dangerous prisoner who is about to be released and getting involved with his estranged family once more

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Roger Valentine is paired with Lewis on his return to work and proves to be a bit heavy-handed dealing with a stalker who terrorised a female transsexual, so he's delighted to learn his new partner is prepared to cover for him. programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Charity Wakefield and Hattie Morahan lead the cast in Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel about a voyage of sexual and romantic discovery. Following the death of her husband, Mrs Dashwood and her daughters are forced to give up their home and move to a small cottage in Devonshire. Elinor is disappointed to leave her old home after falling in love with Edward Ferrars, her sister-in-law's brother, but looks forward to a promised visit. Marianne quickly finds she has an admirer in Colonel Brandon but is swept of her feet by neighbour Willoughby

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Colonel Brandon is called away on urgent business, leaving Marianne free to fall blissfully in love with Willoughby - until he too suddenly leaves for London. Meanwhile, Elinor has a disappointing meeting with Edward, but the reasons for his distant behaviour become clear when Sir John's niece Lucy Steele entrusts her with a shocking secret

programme.title: Sense and Sensibility
programme.desc: Marianne's health deteriorates as Colonel Brandon reveals the full extent of Willoughby's betrayal. Meanwhile, Edward states his intentions to honour his engagement to Lucy Steele, despite being disinherited by his mother as a result. Charity Wakefield and Dominic Cooper star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor's continuing campaign against bureaucrats finds another victim - a health and safety inspector who comes to check on the compost heap at the bottom of the Meldrews' allotment. Christmas special from 1991, with guest star Eric Idle

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: It is work experience day, but Bren cannot show the new girl a typical routine in the canteen because the new boy has blocked the front of the cooker with his ladder - which the bread delivery woman refuses to go under. Canteen comedy, with Victoria Wood, Thelma Barlow, Sue Cleaver and Shobna Gulati

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Jean vents her anger on the customers, while Tony considers asking Bren to accompany him on a trip to Marbella. Petula returns with interesting news, and Anita celebrates the second week of her relationship with Pedro. Victoria Wood stars

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Bren is so excited about her forthcoming holiday with Tony she even considers buying some new clothes, and while a forlorn Stan tells everyone his sad news, Petula gets a little hot under the collar in her caravan. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Julie Walters

programme.desc: The local Blainthorp Music Festival celebrates its 50th birthday, bringing with it fierce competition. programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Nurse Crane and Trixie investigate when a mother abandons her children at the clinic, fearing they were being abused or neglected. Her husband admits that she has been behaving strangely lately, and when she is referred to a neurologist by Dr Turner, the truth proves devastating for the whole family. Christopher admits that Alexandra has been unhappy since his divorce, and Violet and Fred organise a beauty contest to raise money for charity. programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Cameras follow drug raids across Lancashire and beyond. Plus, an impressive racing car is put up for sale at the proceeds-of-crime auction and the tale of how a woman helped police get the better of a romance fraudster in Durham is recounted. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates the scandal of a north London bedsit that's simply not fit for purpose. He also joins the Birmingham empty homes officer who's fighting back against the city's 10,000 empty properties

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool Antique Centre. Host Christina Trevanion finds out about naturalist and painter John James Audubon's Birds of America books, which are on display in the city's Central Library

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Nancy is shocked when Ellen reveals Lawrence's dark secret. Annie is forced to choose between her duty to Harry and her love for Adam, Bea discovers Finch has doubts about her motives for marrying Billy, and Tucker's murderous revenge has devastating consequences for all four of the women. Drama, starring Summer Strallen and Nathaniel Parker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explain how necessity often forced cooks to make the most of limited ingredients to create tasty dishes. They reveal that many popular meals were invented during hard times, from mince and dumplings to Scotch broths

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Cornwall on a property search with a couple who have £750,000 to realise their dream of starting their married life together near the Cornish coast. While in the area, Nicki visits Looe Harbour to learn about the importance of sustainable fishing methods, which is championed by the local fishmongers she meets

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Joanna, Robert and their son Morgan have just moved into their brand-new house in Wrexham. On finding the prospect of sorting out the bare garden quite daunting they call upon Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich to help clear the builder's rubble and provide some ideas. The family has a £6,500 budget to create a garden from scratch that delivers a real 'wow' factor, incorporating dramatic planting, big architectural features and a swanky hot tub area

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention, spurring restorer Will Kirk into action. Also being given a much-needed makeover are an air-powered toy car and a RNLI donation box. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Rich brothers Harry and David head to Corby, Northamptonshire to meet Keri and Mark. The pair don't get to share too much time together, so though they love the pub, instead of going out they want to create a pub garden at home. Charlie and the siblings assess the pair's boring and featureless plot and come up with ideas as to how it can be turned into a relaxing and tranquil social space for Keri, Mark and their friends. With a whole garden to make over and a proper bar to incorporate, the challenge promises to be a tough task to pull off

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The Browns await the fruits of their fraud after faking Grandad's death to get a payout, but the insurance company starts to grow suspicious. Agnes struggles to keep him out of sight, as she has promised the money to Dermot and Maria, who are desperate to get a place of their own

programme.title: Zodiac (2007)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime thriller about the tortuous hunt for a mysterious serial killer who terrorised San Francisco in the late 1960s and early 1970s, and taunted the police and media with cryptic messages. Three men became obsessed with identifying the murderer after his reign of terror inexplicably stopped, but their fixation with the case took a heavy toll on their lives. Starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Robert Downey Jr, Mark Ruffalo and Brian Cox

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Football Focus

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

Plus, presenter Eric Knowles pays a visit to the Museum of Timekeeping in nearby Upton to find out about a local man's mission to save the St Pancras Railway Clock

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A newly engaged couple are joined by Jules Hudson on a house-hunting mission in south Worcestershire, looking to purchase their first joint home with a budget of £300,000

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland. With Dion Dublin and Tim Cahill

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a sensory garden for two autistic girls in Aylesbury, while also providing a garden in Liphook for Anne, Shaun and daughter Joanne

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a mortgage advisor, a mother and daughter, and a print manager

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a music edition of the celebrity general knowledge quiz, with contestants YolanDa Brown, JB Gill, Dan Donovan, Brix Smith Start, Patti Boulaye, Jess Conrad, Dave Bartram and Blaze Bayley trying to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Katie Derham hosts the climax of the classical music festival, live from the Royal Albert Hall, drawing a unique season of live performances to a celebratory close. Soprano Golda Schultz and violinist Lisa Batiashvili join Dalia Stasevska, the BBC Symphony Orchestra and the BBC Singers in a programme that includes music from Mozart's Marriage of Figaro, a world premiere from Andrea Tarrodi, music by Richard Strauss, Stephen Sondheim's A Little Night Music, and The Lark Ascending by Vaughan Williams. The night comes to a jubilant close with all the traditional favourites, including the Fantasia on British Sea-Songs, You'll Never Walk Alone, Rule, Britannia, Pomp and Circumstance March No 1, and Jerusalem

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker reunite behind their famous desk to reminisce about their favourite comedy moments from their long-running TV shows. Again they present a selection of monologues, musical numbers and sketches, including the classic Fork Handles

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the opening fixtures of the Premier League season, including Liverpool v Leeds United at Anfield and Fulham v Arsenal at Craven Cottage. Leeds were playing their first top-flight match since 2004, but could not have faced a sterner test against the champions, who last lost in the league at Anfield in April 2017. Meanwhile, Fulham also faced a tough challenge on their return to the league as they took on their London rivals. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to look back at the opening match of the season between Kansas City Chiefs and Houston Texans and preview the rest of the week one fixtures

programme.title: At Any Price (2012)
programme.desc: A farmer strives to grow his family business by any means necessary, but his rebellious youngest son proves a constant disappointment, more interested in pursuing a career as a racing driver than helping with the farm. When a series of crises threaten the family's future, father and son must learn to put aside their differences to avert the disaster. Drama, starring Dennis Quaid and Zac Efron

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the opening fixtures of the Premier League season, including Liverpool v Leeds United at Anfield and Fulham v Arsenal at Craven Cottage. Leeds were playing their first top-flight match since 2004, but could not have faced a sterner test against the champions, who last lost in the league at Anfield in April 2017. Meanwhile, Fulham also faced a tough challenge on their return to the league as they took on their London rivals. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: A Great North Run Celebration
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents a look back at highlights of through the years on the day the 40th staging of the iconic half-marathon was scheduled to take place. Jeanette Kwakye meets some of those who would have been running this year, including NHS staff, and the achievements of those who have raised money for charity are celebrated

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters explore a bungalow in the village of Chelsfield and a semi-detached house in Erith, both in the south east of London. Plus, a shop with plenty of flats above

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Stamford Meadows in Lincolnshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Grimsthorpe castle to find out about its royal collection

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in Plymouth, where 400 years ago a group of Christians boarded the Mayflower for a journey across the Atlantic that changed American history. With music by Seth Lakeman and Russell Watson

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore finds a pair of seats, an Edwardian armchair and an old typewriter at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Castleford Tigers v Hull FC (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens. The match was originally scheduled for August, but had to be postponed when a number of Hull players and staff tested positive for Covid-19, but now they have recovered the team will have their eyes on the trophy they won back-to-back in 2016 and 2017. The Tigers are looking to win this trophy for the first time since 1986 and avenge a 28-12 defeat at the hands of the Airlie Birds at this stage of last season's competition

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson assists a couple who have a £500,000 budget and a dream of leaving their city life in Bristol behind them and finding their perfect country home in Monmouthshire. While in the county, keen archaeologist Jules visits the village of Trellech, where local excavation efforts are unearthing what could be the largest medieval settlement in Wales.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are in Stoke-on-Trent to learn about an ambitious scheme to bring the countryside into the heart of the city. Margherita finds out what it takes to move a stretch of the River Trent, while Matt meets the next generation of nature lovers as he glories in mud with some of the city's youngest residents and their parents. In the light of the coronavirus pandemic, Tom Heap investigates whether the UK is sufficiently armed against further threats of diseases that spread from animals to humans, and Adam Henson meets another set of regional competitors vying to be crowned national One Man and His Dog champion

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain and the Blitz. Combining stories from all over the country with a special event filmed at Biggin Hill Airfield, this episode tells the story of a defining moment in Britain's war, through the people and objects who were there. Fiona begins on board the iconic Supermarine Spitfire, flying over the Weald of Kent. Then, she and the team uncover the stories from family mementos, including a scramble bell that got the pilots into the air, ops room models that tracked their every move and a steering column from a British fighter shot down in combat

programme.title: Extinction: The Facts
programme.desc: With a million species at threat, David Attenborough explores the science of extinction and how this has consequences for everyone. The programme examines why biodiversity loss is happening and what it means - not just for the natural world, but for all mankind. The loss of biodiversity threatens food and water security, undermines humans' ability to control the climate, and places humans at greater risk of pandemics. Leading experts including Sir Robert Watson and plant scientist Prof Kathy Willis explain how all groups in the natural world are in decline, and that extinction is now happening up to 100 times faster than the natural evolutionary rate

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Robin meets Raphael for a drink, and he explains more about Freddie's bullying of Rhiannon, which gives Geraint a strong motive for Chiswell's murder. They are interrupted by a call from Matthew, which Robin blocks before revealing that they have separated. The police show Robin and Strike CCTV footage of Kinvara at Paddington Station the night before Chiswell died, while Tegan reveals that she previously witnessed Kinvara hit Chiswell over the head with a hammer. Crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling that chronicle cases investigated by private detective Cormoran Strike. Starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Micah Richards to review the latest Premier League fixtures on the opening weekend of the season, featuring West Bromwich Albion v Leicester City at the Hawthorns and Tottenham Hotspur v Everton at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: After losing their opening two games of the season, West Ham United's women's team are preparing to take on their bogey team Manchester City, who are packed full of England superstars. Within their own team, club captain Gilly Flaherty starts thinking about what life might be like when her football career comes to an end, French superstar Kenza Dali opens up about her mother's illness, and there is a rare appearance on the bench for academy player Victoria - who is still working hard on her GCSE exams. Narrated by Jaime Winstone

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Arit Anderson try to bring an Alpine-cum-Oktoberfest element to a couple's garden in Chesham, Buckinghamshire, to give Munich-born wife Lisa a taste of home

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps an aspiring writer from London to find a new home in Dumfries and Galloway, and also heads for the hills on a Scottish gold rush

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea welcomes 93-year-old Dora Paul to the workshop, along with the unusual item she has brought in for repair - an old tram seat which once ferried passengers on the number 81 tram in Salford, Greater Manchester. Jay Blades and the team also tackle a vintage horse-racing game and two stained-glass panels

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz in which one contestant is put in control and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which of the other players is best suited to answer

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: In Norfolk, IT man Dominic faces off against train driver Martin. Paul Martin teaches them the tricks of the trade and turns them into successful antiques dealers

programme.title: In Which We Serve (1942)
programme.desc: When a British destroyer is torpedoed during the Battle of Crete, surviving crew members are left clinging to the wreckage and recall their previous adventures at sea - and the reasons why they enlisted for service in the first place. Second World War drama, starring Noel Coward, who co-directed with David Lean, alongside John Mills, Bernard Miles, Celia Johnson and Richard Attenborough

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Birmingham's factories were crucial to war production, and though the city was heavily bombed during the Second World War, much of the destruction was kept secret. Hollywood actor David Harewood takes an aerial journey over his home city to see the land from the perspective of the German pilots and talks to survivors on the ground about their recollections of the attacks

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The elephant and mole rat start their lives looking ancient and remain that way into old age, yet they outlive other animals their size. The veteran broadcaster considers the virtues of both creatures

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts touch down at Bletchley Park, the Ulster Transport Museum and Crowcombe Court to track down some unusual items to sell at auction. They uncover a beautiful watercolour and some stunning porcelain. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits Antony in Cornwall to unearth a fascinating family history

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radio host Dev Griffin, comedian Alex Horne, presenter June Sarpong and the lovely Debbie McGee go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: At the Moreton-in-Marsh Show in the Cotswolds, Betty Judge shows 21 of her pygmy goats, while Laura Ketley exhibits her best dairy Toggenburg goats

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actors Bill Simons and Derek Fowlds hunt for antiques in Helmsley, North Yorkshire, before concluding with an auction in Ilkley, West Yorkshire. Bill's keen eye for unusual items secures him some unexpected lots, while Derek charms his way to great discounts. But will the antique hunt grind to a halt when the classic car breaks down?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reaps harvest from his vegetable garden, plants up a seasonal pot and gives a masterclass on how to make the perfect compost. Adam Frost travels to York to meet a couple who took on 20 acres of land and filled it with flowers, and in Walsall cameras visit a garden planted with exotic palms and foliage that has made a positive contribution to the owner's mental health. Plus, another chance to see a collection of late summer flowering bergamot plants in Wales and the zoologist in Lancashire who has spent his life exploring the microscopic insects that live in the soil

programme.title: The Romantics and Us with Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian explores the enduring and powerful legacy the Romantics have left on the modern world. In this episode, Simon explores how Romantic artists created the secular icons of the modern democratic age, whose power continues to inspire the passions of revolt to this day. He considers the elixir of rebellion and the idea - so powerful in the words and images of William Blake - that imaginative passion can conquer mechanical logic and can create an art for the people. With contributions from actors Harriet Walter and Christopher Eccleston, hip-hop artist Testament and the French street artist PBoy

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert joins a crew of road workers in south Wales to find out what maintaining the highways and byways is really like. All the signs point to appalling weather, a risky environment and back-breaking labour, but if he can get the team to 'dig' his digging, can road-work-rookie Gilbert bag himself the best seat on the job?

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Unsure until they share their responses, Tom and Louise discuss the rekindling of their relationship. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the opening match of the three-game series, which came from Emirates Old Trafford

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: First episode of the sci-fi drama reinventing the cult 1970s series, starring Edward James Olmos as starship commander William Adama, charged with leading an attempt to find Earth. When Galactica is contacted by the Olympic Carrier, a vessel with more than 1,300 crew, Cdr Adama is advised there is a traitor on board. With Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Boomer wakes up soaking wet, covered in red stains and carrying an explosive - and six more bombs are missing from the locker. Unable to remember where she has been or what she has been doing, she believes that her body is being controlled by some kind of exterior force, possibly intending her to sabotage Galactica's water supply against her will

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For any parent, making sure a child gets the best schooling is often a worry, but when your child has complex special educational needs or disabilities, it can cause real anxiety. As most children in England return to their classrooms, reporter Sean Dilley investigates the system for supporting young people with special educational needs. He meets families who, during lockdown, struggled without any support at all, and now, as their children head back to school, fear they may not get the right support to help them learn and stay safe in their classrooms. Sean discovers an adversarial system that was supposed to put the needs of children at its heart, but instead has created what some call 'a treacle of bureaucracy' for parents to navigate

programme.title: The Unbelievable Story of Carl Beech
programme.desc: Vanessa Engle's documentary telling the story of the man who claimed he had been sexually abused by a group of prominent men in the 1970s and '80s, naming Jimmy Savile, ex-prime minister Edward Heath and former home secretary Leon Brittan as among those involved. The film is constructed using Beech's own words, taken from media and police interviews and from his blogs, and there are interviews with those who were close to the story at the time, including his ex-wife and Diana Clemetson, the widow of Lord Brittan

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team builds a 'plane den' for 11-year-old Philip, in the hope that a place where he can indulge his fascination with the RAF will help him cope with his digestive illness

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim and Naomi present strange stories from the natural world. This time they check out some wild weather, including a storm of giant hailstones at a sunny beach, a terrifying tornado made of fire and the weird winds that help to keep the Amazon rainforest growing

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: The most unusual places in the UK

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Amazing animals and an epic first challenge for the newest presenter

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Highlights of the series in which Steve Backshall undertook a global quest from the Arctic Circle to Antarctica, encountering the world's deadliest animals in their natural habitats. Featuring the presenter's encounters with polar bears and leopard seals, his failed attempts to stand up to tornado-force winds and scorching-hot lava flows, and previously unseen footage of his bumps, trips and slips

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Iain Stewart examines key moments in the development of Eurasia, which is the combined continental land mass of Europe and Asia and was formed by a series of tectonic-plate collisions over millions of years. In Kerala, a freshwater fish provides evidence that Madagascar was once joined to India, which created the Himalayas as it pushed its way 4,000km north to its present position

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers settle their livestock into winter housing as the first frosts come in. In the Lake District, vet Dani prepares to perform pregnancy tests on all her cows - while six months pregnant herself. The outcome of the tests will determine the future income of the farm. High on the uplands of Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan prepare to undertake a new venture, and in Aberdeenshire, Isla and George fight to save a dangerously dehydrated deer calf

programme.title: The Red Shoes (1948)
programme.desc: A budding prima ballerina is set to achieve her dream of stardom when she decides to leave her prestigious company to marry a composer - a decision that enrages her possessive mentor, who vows revenge. Powell and Pressburger's drama, based on the story by Hans Christian Andersen, starring Anton Walbrook, Moira Shearer, Marius Goring, Robert Helpmann and Albert Basserman

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies Michael Buerk on a trip to his childhood home of Hereford, where his culinary memories range from the unpromising tinned spaghetti and overcooked cabbage served up by his grandmother to the wholesome food he found on his girlfriend's family table

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Daniel O'Donnell back to the islands of Arranmore and Owey off the coast of Co Donegal, where the singer reminisces about a childhood holiday he had there in 1968

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attend questions why spirals are so widespread in nature. He gleans some answers by examining the narwhal's tusk and the spiral shells found on snails and ammonites, as he continues to discover nature's evolutionary anomalies

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Examining how animals, including monkeys, wolves, bears, foxes and birds, cope with mountain life in the Himalayas, and how the harsh terrain has given many creatures remarkable strength. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits some of the great British war films that helped boost the nation's morale in the difficult years that followed the end of the Second World War. Among the silver screen gems highlighted are In Which We Serve, Reach for the Sky, Bridge on the River Kwai and The Dam Busters. The programme includes archive interviews with stars including Noel Coward, Richard Attenborough, David Lean, Alec Guinness, Kenneth More and John Mills, and Sylvia shares her memories of filming her own contribution to the genre, Ice Cold in Alex

programme.title: The Battle of the River Plate (1956)
programme.desc: British naval forces pursue German battleship the Admiral Graf Spee, finally blockading the vessel in Montevideo harbour, where its commanding officer is faced with an impossible choice. Second World War adventure from Powell and Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Anthony Quayle, Peter Finch, Bernard Lee and Jack Gwillim

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a thousand-mile journey from the Swedish capital of Stockholm to Abisko in the northern reaches of the Arctic Circle. In Stockholm, Michael braves a precarious tour of the city from its rooftops before boarding a heritage tram to get the lowdown on 1930s Sweden from an expert. Moving on, he finishes his journey deep in the Arctic Circle at a remote climate research station. He also has lunch with traditional Sami people in Vilhelmina and checks into an ice hotel

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring suspects the residents of Walmington-on-Sea have grown complacent about the threat of invasion, and gets his men to pose as traitors to see how the locals would deal with genuine Nazi infiltrators in their midst. Classic comedy first shown in 1977, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines the reign of Queen Anne, which saw England and Scotland becoming united as Great Britain and rising to become a major European power. However, Anne's skills as a world leader have long been overshadowed by the scandalous tell-all story written by rejected lady-in-waiting Sarah Churchill, which provided the basis of Oscar-winning film The Favourite. Lucy reexamines Sarah's story, and discovers it may not be entirely true

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the high-brow quiz, asking questions about road, rail and other things beginning with the letter R. Trying to come up with the answers are Alan Davies, Aisling Bea, Cally Beaton and Holly Walsh

programme.title: Memento (2000)
programme.desc: An insurance investigator suffers from short-term memory loss after an attack in which his wife was also raped and murdered. Unable to remember events for more than a few minutes, he is determined to track down the killer and relies on copious note-taking and reminders tattooed on his body to aid his mission. Christopher Nolan's thriller, starring Guy Pearce, Carrie-Anne Moss, Joe Pantoliano and Mark Boone Jr

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1980 Big Hits
programme.desc: A selection of performances from the show in the first year of the 1980s, when the rise of a new generation of pop stars signalled a new era for Top of the Pops and for British society. Featuring My Girl by Madness, Brass in Pocket by Pretenders and Geno by Dexys Midnight Runners, as well as appearances by Adam Ant, Spandau Ballet, the Piranhas, Hot Chocolate, OMD, Motorhead and the Beat

programme.title: After the Storm (2016)
programme.desc: Following the death of his father, a private detective struggles to find child support money and reconnect with his son and ex-wife. Comedy drama, starring Hiroshi Abe, Yoko Maki and Satomi Kobayashi. In Japanese

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter G. Lorraine Pascale cooks pan-fried mascarpone gnocchi with dreamy basil pesto, and Rachel Khoo serves grapefruit meringue tartlets

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani goes back to her Yorkshire roots exploring the lesser-known River Burn in the glorious Swinton estate. She discovers why people seem to have fallen in love with fishing since lockdown and how the humble willow is the perfect weapon against riverbank erosion. Meanwhile, Adam Henson meets this year's One Man and His Dog competitors from Northern Ireland and Wales, and John Craven joins Cerys Matthews and Simon King for the 2020 Photographic Competition

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reaps harvest from his vegetable garden, plants up a seasonal pot and gives a masterclass on how to make the perfect compost. Adam Frost travels to York to meet a couple who took on 20 acres of land and filled it with flowers, and in Walsall cameras visit a garden planted with exotic palms and foliage that has made a positive contribution to the owner's mental health. Plus, another chance to see a collection of late summer flowering bergamot plants in Wales and the zoologist in Lancashire who has spent his life exploring the microscopic insects that live in the soil

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: The presenters look to the future. George Anderson plants up a large container with what he calls a 'bulb lasagne', using different kinds of bulbs to produce layers of colour in the spring. Carole Baxter shows different methods of propagating new plants for next year, Chris Beardshaw plants a new border for year-round colour and Kirsty Wilson reveals why green roofs are the next big thing

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the opening episode, Mary heads back to the city where she learnt to cook, Paris, where she visits one of the city's creperies to finesse her technique. Later, inspired by pastries in Parisian patisserie windows, she makes her own brioche and apple frangipane tart

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel around Asia exploring the roots of some of their favourite dishes from the continent. They begin in Hong Kong, where they learn about the versatility of the wok in Cantonese cuisine at a street-food stall, while breakfast reveals a surprising legacy of British rule, before the duo are sent on their way with a traditional Chinese good luck ceremony

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: As a child, Alistair McGowan loved school lunches, especially the puddings. His mother's cooking was equally memorable and innovative, providing plenty of inspiration for the dishes that Brian Turner creates for Alistair. Together they revisit evocative places from Alistair's childhood, from Worcestershire's plum orchards to his old school, where sights and smells take him straight back to his youth. In his childhood home, we discover how Alistair realised his gift for impersonation as he switches effortlessly into some of the voices that helped him make his name

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: Ashley John-Baptiste meets a group of people working to clean up their neighbourhood, and Rev Kate Bottley goes in search of inner peace on a wire high above the cliffs of Anglesey

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Bill Bailey narrates this natural history documentary telling the story of some of nature's most extraordinary partnerships, revealing what sometimes happens when animals need an itch scratching, help getting into food or home security assistance. The answer is, to find a partner - and not necessarily one of the same species. Highlights include hippos joining forces with fish for a full-body exfoliation and ravens inviting wolverines over for some bone-crunching assistance with their dinner

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by Swedish Academy Award-winning actress Ingrid Bergman, exploring the milestones of her life and career, including her appearances in Casablanca and Spellbound. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Casablanca (1942)
programme.desc: The American expat owner of a Moroccan nightclub is reunited with his old flame during the Second World War. Now married to a Czech freedom fighter, she needs his help to get them safely out of the country before they are captured by the Nazis - but the former lovers also have to wrestle the temptation to rekindle their simmering romance. Drama, with Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Claude Rains and Paul Henreid

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Highlights of the past two series of the motoring show, including the time the team took a Yorkshire road trip in the latest sports cars from Ferrari, Aston Martin and Porsche. Plus, Paddy McGuinness building his own luxurious off-roader from scratch and Chris Harris putting the Rolls-Royce Cullinan through its paces

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones cast their eyes over the final batch of business pitches of the current series. Stepping into the arena are an inventor who has come up with an unusual way of controlling household draughts, a businessman who claims there is money to be made in encouraging dogs to eat bugs, a fitness teacher who launches into a shimmying session, and the creator of a high-end garment bag. Evan Davis oversees proceedings as the Dragons decide if any of these pitches are worthy of investment

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for brown trout on the river Test in Hampshire, using a Victorian fly-casting technique pioneered in the area as well as an authentic 19th-century rod. The Mayflies are hatching, and the famously short-lived insects inspire some thoughts on the ephemeral nature of life, and what they would do if they only had 24 hours left

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: Harry Hill casts a comical eye over the history of police dramas, from the early days of Dixon of Dock Green to acclaimed modern shows like Line of Duty. He examines the familiar tropes that audiences know and love, as well as drawing attention to a few that have escaped audience's attention

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker revisits documentaries exploring the commodification of forbidden desires, taking in subjects ranging from the porn industry to the US opioid crisis. He is reunited with porn star JJ, who has turned his back on his old career to begin a new life in eastern Europe, and drug addict Katilia, who is still struggling to turn her life around

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam takes two of her friends to meet with an attorney specialising in divorces, calls Jeff over to help assemble a chinchilla cage, and meets one of Frankie's new friends in surprising circumstances

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam has to persuade a reluctant Phil to give a sample of her DNA, receives some bad news from an orthopaedic specialist, and has a spectacular blow-up with Max

programme.title: Radio 2 Live at Home
programme.desc: Specially recorded performances from and interviews performers

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the second match of the three-game series, which came from Emirates Old Trafford

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Claude begins to teach Peter how to use his powers, and Hiro is pursued by a mysterious figure as he continues his quest to find the sword. Meanwhile, Claire has some success in the hunt for her birth mother and Niki is offered help from a psychiatrist. Guest starring Christopher Eccleston (Doctor Who)

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Hiro attempts to make his father and sister understand his newly discovered powers, and Claire's search for her birth mother leads her to a Texas trailer park. Meanwhile, Peter's faith in those closest to him is tested when Claude shows him how to harness his abilities. Christopher Eccleston, George Takei and Jessalyn Gilsig star

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Most people who go missing are reported to police within a few hours, but for those who live on the streets it isn't always so straightforward. Aaron hasn't been seen for two months when the homeless healthcare centre that usually treats him notifies Gloucestershire Constabulary that he is a missing person. Officers scour the streets and homeless shelters but can find no trace of him. The town's rough sleepers know him well, but no one has any idea what may have happened to him. It's not long before another drug user is added to the missing priority list - James, who is of no fixed address, has discharged himself from hospital against medical advice. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Young, Gifted and Classical: The Making of a Maestro
programme.desc: Documentary about cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason, the first black performer to be named BBC Young Musician of the Year, and his six musically gifted siblings. The programme explores issues of diversity in the world of classical music and culminates in Sheku's biggest performance to date at London's Royal Festival Hall, appearing alongside Chineke!, an orchestra composed entirely of ethnic minority musicians

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Siblings Sheku and Isata Kanneh-Mason take to the stage for their first Prom together to present an evening of chamber music for cello and piano. Featuring works by Beethoven, Rachmaninov, American composer Samuel Barber, and English composer Frank Bridge, the brother and sister pairing bring their unique musical chemistry to the Royal Albert Hall. Presented by Tom Service, with guest Joanna MacGregor

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: Memories of the late '60s and early '70s, a period when inequality, poverty and racism fanned the flames of radical black politics and resulted in a harder soul sound. Stax Records went from being a label built around an integrated house band to becoming a black-centric business spearheaded by Isaac Hayes' expansive, flamboyant soul symphonies. Meanwhile, the Temptations tackled socially aware subjects like the Detroit riot and absent fathers, while Marvin Gaye conceived the epic What's Going On. Narrated by Carleen Anderson, with contributions from Mavis Staples, David Porter, Al Bell, Otis Williams, Barrett Strong and Fred Wesley

programme.title: Motown at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of studio performances by some of Motown's greatest artists, originally broadcast to mark the record label's 50th anniversary. Featuring Diana Ross and the Supremes, the Temptations, Smokey Robinson and the Miracles, the Jackson Five, the Four Tops and Stevie Wonder

programme.title: BBC Proms 2017
programme.desc: At London's Royal Albert Hall, Jools Holland pays tribute to Stax Records, alongside original stars Eddie Floyd, Sam Moore, William Bell, Booker T Jones and Steve Cropper, plus UK artists including Tom Jones, James Morrison, Beverley Knight, Nadia Rose and Sweetie Irie, and Ruby Turner. Founded in 1957, the Memphis-based company was synonymous with Southern Soul, a distinctive blend of funk, gospel and R'n'B, producing soul artists such as Otis Redding, Carla Thomas, and Sam and Dave. Music includes Green Onions, Knock on Wood, Soul Man and Try a Little Tenderness

programme.title: Gregory Porter's Popular Voices
programme.desc: The soul and jazz performer explores the soft, intimate art of crooning. Born with the arrival of the microphone in the 1930s, crooning was initially about men seducing women and thrived through signature stars such as Bing Crosby, Nat King Cole and Frank Sinatra. However, far from disappearing with the advent of rock 'n' roll, the art of crooning gained a new existential edge and was transformed by the likes of Roy Orbison, David Bowie and even Lana Del Rey into a haunting and abiding strain of contemporary pop. Contributors include Iggy Pop, Joshua Homme, George Benson and Beck

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: Memories of the late '60s and early '70s, a period when inequality, poverty and racism fanned the flames of radical black politics and resulted in a harder soul sound. Stax Records went from being a label built around an integrated house band to becoming a black-centric business spearheaded by Isaac Hayes' expansive, flamboyant soul symphonies. Meanwhile, the Temptations tackled socially aware subjects like the Detroit riot and absent fathers, while Marvin Gaye conceived the epic What's Going On. Narrated by Carleen Anderson, with contributions from Mavis Staples, David Porter, Al Bell, Otis Williams, Barrett Strong and Fred Wesley

programme.title: Young, Gifted and Classical: The Making of a Maestro
programme.desc: Documentary about cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason, the first black performer to be named BBC Young Musician of the Year, and his six musically gifted siblings. The programme explores issues of diversity in the world of classical music and culminates in Sheku's biggest performance to date at London's Royal Festival Hall, appearing alongside Chineke!, an orchestra composed entirely of ethnic minority musicians

programme.title: How We Tamed the Cat and Dog
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary examining archaeology and genetics to answer questions about cats and dogs, including where they came from and how they ended up living with humans. This first edition focuses on the dog, revealing that the emotional bond between human and canine is so profound that it is helping transform the lives of hardened criminals in the US prison system convicted of the most violent crimes. The programme also hears from Callie Truelove, a girl living with the rare genetic condition Williams syndrome, which makes her extremely loving and sociable. Geneticists have discovered that the same mutations that give Callie her super social nature have also been found in dogs. Narrated by Mark Benton

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin and crew set off across the Sudan, but an unreliable rail service and gun-toting bandits ensure their journey is one to remember. Unable to venture into Zaire or Ethiopia, they negotiate a narrow corridor through the civil war zone and reflect on one of the journey's most difficult stretches

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: When an unknown bomber threatens to blow up a chemical plant, Saga and Martin team up once again to track down the villain. After compiling the clues, they find a common denominator that confines the would-be terrorist to a particular area, but the questions still outweigh the answers. Meanwhile, Saga begins to realise romance is harder than she thought, and Martin uncovers a secret in his Swedish counterpart's past. Crime drama, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Laura, the key witness in Saga and Martin's ongoing case, regains consciousness, and finally manages to help the duo compile a composite sketch of the perpetrator. However, it becomes clear that an insider is doctoring information in their reports. Scandinavian crime drama, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Armed with a composite sketch of the perpetrator, Saga and Martin finally believe they are about to bring their suspect to justice. However, one final mystery remains, and as a chain of leads takes the duo to Kastrup Airport, they begin to realise they might be too late. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The Danish and Swedish police forces bring their investigation to a close, but it soon becomes clear that the case has not yet reached its conclusion. As a race to prevent another disaster gets under way, Martin makes a crucial decision, and Saga if forced to make a difficult choice that could change everything. Scandinavian crime drama, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: Kate Bush at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of the singer's performances at the BBC's studios between 1978 and 1994. Bush appeared on a variety of programmes, including Top of the Pops, Wogan, Ask Aspel, Saturday Night at the Mill and the Leo Sayer Show. Featuring the hits Wuthering Heights, Babooshka, Running Up That Hill and Hounds of Love, as well as intriguing and lesser-known material

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin and crew set off across the Sudan, but an unreliable rail service and gun-toting bandits ensure their journey is one to remember. Unable to venture into Zaire or Ethiopia, they negotiate a narrow corridor through the civil war zone and reflect on one of the journey's most difficult stretches

programme.title: How We Tamed the Cat and Dog
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary examining archaeology and genetics to answer questions about cats and dogs, including where they came from and how they ended up living with humans. This first edition focuses on the dog, revealing that the emotional bond between human and canine is so profound that it is helping transform the lives of hardened criminals in the US prison system convicted of the most violent crimes. The programme also hears from Callie Truelove, a girl living with the rare genetic condition Williams syndrome, which makes her extremely loving and sociable. Geneticists have discovered that the same mutations that give Callie her super social nature have also been found in dogs. Narrated by Mark Benton

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car

programme.title: Jet! When Britain Ruled the Skies
programme.desc: Two-part documentary examining advances in British aviation in the 1950s and 60s, which begins by exploring the design and technology behind a new generation of military planes including the Vulcan bomber and the Meteor jet fighter. The first programme investigates how the achievements came at a time of national austerity, and there is a tragic reminder of how some test pilots paid the ultimate price for their work. Including contributions by Norman Tebbit and Tony Blackman

programme.title: Battlefield Britain
programme.desc: The Snows use state-of-the-art graphics to re-create the Battle of Britain, one of the pivotal conflicts of the Second World War. Peter explains the role of radar in detecting Luftwaffe planes as they headed for Dover, while Dan experiences the physiological effects of the high G-forces that would have been felt by the RAF pilots

programme.title: Plague Fiction
programme.desc: In light of the Covid-19 global outbreak, Professor Laura Ashe takes a look back at the Black Death of the 14th century, the deadliest pandemic recorded in human history. Going through the literature of the time, from one of the earliest accounts in 1347, to the great Plague Diary of Samuel Pepys, Professor Ashe explores how literature helped us cope with fear and tragedy, the importance of bravery and personal sacrifice, and whether the words of the past can offer us the comfort and healing that we need now

programme.title: Birth of the British Novel
programme.desc: Author and critic Henry Hitchings explores the evolution of the novel in 18th-century Britain, and offers his view that it was a time of cultural revolution. He examines the social and political history of the period, uses paintings by great artists to illustrate scenes from key novels, and explains how major genres from light entertainment for female readers to political thrillers were perfected. Along the way, he meets authors including Martin Amis, Will Self, Tom McCarthy and Jenny Uglow

programme.title: Dancing in the Blitz: How World War Two Made British Ballet
programme.desc: Documentary in which David Bintley, director of the Birmingham Royal Ballet, explores how British ballet thrived during the war. Using rare and previously unseen archive footage, Bintley charts how the then Sadler's Wells Ballet Company, led by Ninette de Valois and featuring stars such as Margot Fonteyn and Frederick Ashton, survived an encounter with Nazi forces in the Netherlands. He reveals how they danced while the bombs fell during the Blitz to bring ballet to the public as an antidote to the austerity the nation faced - before emerging post-war as the Royal Ballet. Featuring contributions by Beryl Grey, Gillian Lynne and Julia Farron
