programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is painting and accidentally paints a rainbow. Meanwhile, Justin and his friend are putting on a colourful show at a theatre

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: The presenter goes back in time to search for a hippo-like reptile called a placerias, to make a clay model of its head to replace one which has broken at the museum

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how car washes work and visits a factory to see how umbrellas are made

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: A very sleepy Tommy Brock is hoisted up into the Squirrel Camp, so Peter and friends try to work out how to get the snoozing badger back down to earth before he wakes up

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran fix up Gran Gran's old bike and cycle through the park together.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's Jess's 'bring-a-pet' birthday party and Waffle competes with Pickles the parrot to be the star. Waffle has a surprise for everyone when he takes to the stage with Simon

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne meets Bella at a city farm as they prepare to take Lemmy, one of their rabbits, to the vet to have his overgrown teeth looked at. Rory meets a vet at a zoo and they examine a corn snake who has had an operation

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers have fun on a walk to a pumpkin patch!

programme.title: At Home with Mr Tumble
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is at home, performing magic tricks.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Kari ignores a sign saying No Ball Games

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: Animation bringing to life the African fable of how a wildebeest stampede gave the crocodile its distinctive bumpy back

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads to South America in search of an armadillo. Along the way, he bumps into a pair of squabbling guanaco and meets a family of mara, a type of giant rodent

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly promises to return a missing doll to its owner, but how can she find out where the owner lives? James helps out, and they make a surprising discovery

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Uncle Matthew and Katie Morag make a new friend when a balloon lands on the island

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: After Sarah helps Scarf Lady prepare the wool for her new roof, she settles down for a dreamy sleep

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The weather is boiling hot, so Lola, Charlie and Arnold think of ways they can cool down

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: When Mo wakes up with a cold, who s there to take over mum duties? Tee of course!

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Mother Clanger closes her garden to visitors to get a bit of peace. She never imagined the problems this would cause for the Soup Dragon and for Major Clanger

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines go for a long walk around the garden, but fall asleep in Upsy Daisy's bed. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Olympic gold medal-winning heptathlete Jessica Ennis-Hill reads Brian Moses' The Frog Olympics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits Wales to take a behind-the-scenes look at the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, revealing how its crew members keep people safe on the seas. Plus, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a fairly modern two-bedroom flat in Manchester, there is a revisit to a property in Bexleyheath, Kent, that was first seen in 2012, and Dion Dublin is in Wolverhampton examining a semi-detached house

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Cameras follow drug raids across Lancashire and beyond. Plus, an impressive racing car is put up for sale at the proceeds-of-crime auction and the tale of how a woman helped police get the better of a romance fraudster in Durham is recounted. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool Antique Centre. Host Christina Trevanion finds out about naturalist and painter John James Audubon's Birds of America books, which are on display in the city's Central Library

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Nancy is shocked when Ellen reveals Lawrence's dark secret. Annie is forced to choose between her duty to Harry and her love for Adam, Bea discovers Finch has doubts about her motives for marrying Billy, and Tucker's murderous revenge has devastating consequences for all four of the women. Drama, starring Summer Strallen and Nathaniel Parker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explain how necessity often forced cooks to make the most of limited ingredients to create tasty dishes. They reveal that many popular meals were invented during hard times, from mince and dumplings to Scotch broths

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Cornwall on a property search with a couple who have £750,000 to realise their dream of starting their married life together near the Cornish coast. While in the area, Nicki visits Looe Harbour to learn about the importance of sustainable fishing methods, which is championed by the local fishmongers she meets

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Joanna, Robert and their son Morgan have just moved into their brand-new house in Wrexham. On finding the prospect of sorting out the bare garden quite daunting they call upon Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich to help clear the builder's rubble and provide some ideas. The family has a £6,500 budget to create a garden from scratch that delivers a real 'wow' factor, incorporating dramatic planting, big architectural features and a swanky hot tub area

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention, spurring restorer Will Kirk into action. Also being given a much-needed makeover are an air-powered toy car and a RNLI donation box. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer discovers the forgotten story of how Wales once led the world in copper production. He begins his quest in Anglesey on Parys Mountain, once the world's biggest copper mine, heading beneath its Mars-like landscape into the miles of tunnels that were first constructed in the 18th century. Then in south Wales, Will unearths the remains of Swansea's once huge copper industry, tracking down the final relics of the lost industrial world of Copperopolis. Later, Will cave-dives through a 19th-century flooded mine to discover its important role in Wales' industrial revolution

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian tries his hand at being a wedding planner. His task is to organise the Hollywood-themed big day of a young couple from the Welsh Valleys - but the bride is not fond of surprises, so what will she make of his chaotic contributions to cake design, music selection, ice sculpture and celebrity recruitment?

programme.title: Zodiac (2007)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime thriller about the tortuous hunt for a mysterious serial killer who terrorised San Francisco in the late 1960s and early 1970s, and taunted the police and media with cryptic messages. Three men became obsessed with identifying the murderer after his reign of terror inexplicably stopped, but their fixation with the case took a heavy toll on their lives. Starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Robert Downey Jr, Mark Ruffalo and Brian Cox

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Paul Ainsworth, along with special guest Charlotte Ritchie. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the first episode, Nadiya kicks things off with a series of her favourite recipes for classic bakes with a twist, including a blueberry scone pizza, and a spicy Asian take on a traditional toad-in-the-hole

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Alex Scott and Tim Cahill look ahead to the opening round of Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Leeds United and Tottenham Hotspur v Everton

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two Grade II-listed cottages in Bassaleg, south Wales and Canterbury, Kent, and a mid-terrace house in Wallasey, Merseyside

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe guide two teams scouring Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire for bargains to take to auction. The reds show David how to be cool, while the blues go in search of a duck-billed platypus. Plus, presenter Eric Knowles pays a visit to the Museum of Timekeeping in nearby Upton to find out about a local man's mission to save the St Pancras Railway Clock

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an active couple return to their roots in Shropshire, looking for a property within their £350,000 budget that gives them the great outdoors on their doorstep

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne calls on help to exercise creative flair to an outdated armchair, while blacksmiths Bex and Dave are tasked with turning old scaffolding props into something special

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer discovers the forgotten story of how Wales once led the world in copper production. He begins his quest in Anglesey on Parys Mountain, once the world's biggest copper mine, heading beneath its Mars-like landscape into the miles of tunnels that were first constructed in the 18th century. Then in south Wales, Will unearths the remains of Swansea's once huge copper industry, tracking down the final relics of the lost industrial world of Copperopolis. Later, Will cave-dives through a 19th-century flooded mine to discover its important role in Wales' industrial revolution

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland. With Dion Dublin and Tim Cahill

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a sensory garden for two autistic girls in Aylesbury, while also providing a garden in Liphook for Anne, Shaun and daughter Joanne

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a mortgage advisor, a mother and daughter, and a print manager

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a music edition of the celebrity general knowledge quiz, with contestants YolanDa Brown, JB Gill, Dan Donovan, Brix Smith Start, Patti Boulaye, Jess Conrad, Dave Bartram and Blaze Bayley trying to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Katie Derham hosts the climax of the classical music festival, live from the Royal Albert Hall, drawing a unique season of live performances to a celebratory close. Soprano Golda Schultz and violinist Lisa Batiashvili join Dalia Stasevska, the BBC Symphony Orchestra and the BBC Singers in a programme that includes music from Mozart's Marriage of Figaro, a world premiere from Andrea Tarrodi, music by Richard Strauss, Stephen Sondheim's A Little Night Music, and The Lark Ascending by Vaughan Williams. The night comes to a jubilant close with all the traditional favourites, including the Fantasia on British Sea-Songs, You'll Never Walk Alone, Rule, Britannia, Pomp and Circumstance March No 1, and Jerusalem

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker reunite behind their famous desk to reminisce about their favourite comedy moments from their long-running TV shows. Again they present a selection of monologues, musical numbers and sketches, including the classic Fork Handles

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the opening fixtures of the Premier League season, including Liverpool v Leeds United at Anfield and Fulham v Arsenal at Craven Cottage. Leeds were playing their first top-flight match since 2004, but could not have faced a sterner test against the champions, who last lost in the league at Anfield in April 2017. Meanwhile, Fulham also faced a tough challenge on their return to the league as they took on their London rivals. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to look back at the opening match of the season between Kansas City Chiefs and Houston Texans and preview the rest of the week one fixtures

programme.title: At Any Price (2012)
programme.desc: A farmer strives to grow his family business by any means necessary, but his rebellious youngest son proves a constant disappointment, more interested in pursuing a career as a racing driver than helping with the farm. When a series of crises threaten the family's future, father and son must learn to put aside their differences to avert the disaster. Drama, starring Dennis Quaid and Zac Efron

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the opening fixtures of the Premier League season, including Liverpool v Leeds United at Anfield and Fulham v Arsenal at Craven Cottage. Leeds were playing their first top-flight match since 2004, but could not have faced a sterner test against the champions, who last lost in the league at Anfield in April 2017. Meanwhile, Fulham also faced a tough challenge on their return to the league as they took on their London rivals. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales

programme.title: A Great North Run Celebration
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents a look back at highlights of through the years on the day the 40th staging of the iconic half-marathon was scheduled to take place. Jeanette Kwakye meets some of those who would have been running this year, including NHS staff, and the achievements of those who have raised money for charity are celebrated

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters explore a bungalow in the village of Chelsfield and a semi-detached house in Erith, both in the south east of London. Plus, a shop with plenty of flats above

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Stamford Meadows in Lincolnshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Grimsthorpe castle to find out about its royal collection

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in Plymouth, where 400 years ago a group of Christians boarded the Mayflower for a journey across the Atlantic that changed American history. With music by Seth Lakeman and Russell Watson

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore finds a pair of seats, an Edwardian armchair and an old typewriter at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Castleford Tigers v Hull FC (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens. The match was originally scheduled for August, but had to be postponed when a number of Hull players and staff tested positive for Covid-19, but now they have recovered the team will have their eyes on the trophy they won back-to-back in 2016 and 2017. The Tigers are looking to win this trophy for the first time since 1986 and avenge a 28-12 defeat at the hands of the Airlie Birds at this stage of last season's competition

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: Three families experience the hardships of the 1920s, giving up all the modern conveniences - microwave, mobile phone, cars and fast food - for life in a mining community. Narrated by Nicola Reynolds

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are in Stoke-on-Trent to learn about an ambitious scheme to bring the countryside into the heart of the city. Margherita finds out what it takes to move a stretch of the River Trent, while Matt meets the next generation of nature lovers as he glories in mud with some of the city's youngest residents and their parents. In the light of the coronavirus pandemic, Tom Heap investigates whether the UK is sufficiently armed against further threats of diseases that spread from animals to humans, and Adam Henson meets another set of regional competitors vying to be crowned national One Man and His Dog champion

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain and the Blitz. Combining stories from all over the country with a special event filmed at Biggin Hill Airfield, this episode tells the story of a defining moment in Britain's war, through the people and objects who were there. Fiona begins on board the iconic Supermarine Spitfire, flying over the Weald of Kent. Then, she and the team uncover the stories from family mementos, including a scramble bell that got the pilots into the air, ops room models that tracked their every move and a steering column from a British fighter shot down in combat

programme.title: Extinction: The Facts
programme.desc: With a million species at threat, David Attenborough explores the science of extinction and how this has consequences for everyone. The programme examines why biodiversity loss is happening and what it means - not just for the natural world, but for all mankind. The loss of biodiversity threatens food and water security, undermines humans' ability to control the climate, and places humans at greater risk of pandemics. Leading experts including Sir Robert Watson and plant scientist Prof Kathy Willis explain how all groups in the natural world are in decline, and that extinction is now happening up to 100 times faster than the natural evolutionary rate

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Robin meets Raphael for a drink, and he explains more about Freddie's bullying of Rhiannon, which gives Geraint a strong motive for Chiswell's murder. They are interrupted by a call from Matthew, which Robin blocks before revealing that they have separated. The police show Robin and Strike CCTV footage of Kinvara at Paddington Station the night before Chiswell died, while Tegan reveals that she previously witnessed Kinvara hit Chiswell over the head with a hammer. Crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling that chronicle cases investigated by private detective Cormoran Strike. Starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Micah Richards to review the latest Premier League fixtures on the opening weekend of the season, featuring West Bromwich Albion v Leicester City at the Hawthorns and Tottenham Hotspur v Everton at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: After losing their opening two games of the season, West Ham United's women's team are preparing to take on their bogey team Manchester City, who are packed full of England superstars. Within their own team, club captain Gilly Flaherty starts thinking about what life might be like when her football career comes to an end, French superstar Kenza Dali opens up about her mother's illness, and there is a rare appearance on the bench for academy player Victoria - who is still working hard on her GCSE exams. Narrated by Jaime Winstone

programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Rich brothers Harry and David head to Corby, Northamptonshire to meet Keri and Mark. Charlie and the siblings assess the pair's boring and featureless plot and come up with ideas as to how it can be turned into a relaxing and tranquil social space for Keri, Mark and their friends. With a whole garden to make over and a proper bar to incorporate, the challenge promises to be a tough task to pull off

programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a music edition of the celebrity general knowledge quiz, with contestants YolanDa Brown, JB Gill, Dan Donovan, Brix Smith Start, Patti Boulaye, Jess Conrad, Dave Bartram and Blaze Bayley trying to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Katie Derham hosts the climax of the classical music festival, live from the Royal Albert Hall, drawing a unique season of live performances to a celebratory close. Soprano Golda Schultz and violinist Lisa Batiashvili join Dalia Stasevska, the BBC Symphony Orchestra and the BBC Singers in a programme that includes music from Mozart's Marriage of Figaro, a world premiere from Andrea Tarrodi, music by Richard Strauss, Stephen Sondheim's A Little Night Music, and The Lark Ascending by Vaughan Williams. The night comes to a jubilant close with all the traditional favourites, including the Fantasia on British Sea-Songs, You'll Never Walk Alone, Rule, Britannia, Pomp and Circumstance March No 1, and Jerusalem

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker reunite behind their famous desk to reminisce about their favourite comedy moments from their long-running TV shows. Again they present a selection of monologues, musical numbers and sketches, including the classic Fork Handles

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the opening fixtures of the Premier League season, including Liverpool v Leeds United at Anfield and Fulham v Arsenal at Craven Cottage. Leeds were playing their first top-flight match since 2004, but could not have faced a sterner test against the champions, who last lost in the league at Anfield in April 2017. Meanwhile, Fulham also faced a tough challenge on their return to the league as they took on their London rivals. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland introduces highlights from the Scottish Premiership, including Rangers v Dundee United, Aberdeen v Kilmarnock and Ross County v Celtic

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to look back at the opening match of the season between Kansas City Chiefs and Houston Texans and preview the rest of the week one fixtures

programme.title: At Any Price (2012)
programme.desc: A farmer strives to grow his family business by any means necessary, but his rebellious youngest son proves a constant disappointment, more interested in pursuing a career as a racing driver than helping with the farm. When a series of crises threaten the family's future, father and son must learn to put aside their differences to avert the disaster. Drama, starring Dennis Quaid and Zac Efron

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the opening fixtures of the Premier League season, including Liverpool v Leeds United at Anfield and Fulham v Arsenal at Craven Cottage. Leeds were playing their first top-flight match since 2004, but could not have faced a sterner test against the champions, who last lost in the league at Anfield in April 2017. Meanwhile, Fulham also faced a tough challenge on their return to the league as they took on their London rivals. With analysis by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: A round-up of political news and events at Westminster and Holyrood.

programme.title: A Great North Run Celebration
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents a look back at highlights of through the years on the day the 40th staging of the iconic half-marathon was scheduled to take place. Jeanette Kwakye meets some of those who would have been running this year, including NHS staff, and the achievements of those who have raised money for charity are celebrated

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters explore a bungalow in the village of Chelsfield and a semi-detached house in Erith, both in the south east of London. Plus, a shop with plenty of flats above

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Mark Stacey and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Stamford Meadows in Lincolnshire, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Grimsthorpe castle to find out about its royal collection

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in Plymouth, where 400 years ago a group of Christians boarded the Mayflower for a journey across the Atlantic that changed American history. With music by Seth Lakeman and Russell Watson

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore finds a pair of seats, an Edwardian armchair and an old typewriter at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Castleford Tigers v Hull FC (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at Totally Wicked Stadium in St Helens. The match was originally scheduled for August, but had to be postponed when a number of Hull players and staff tested positive for Covid-19, but now they have recovered the team will have their eyes on the trophy they won back-to-back in 2016 and 2017. The Tigers are looking to win this trophy for the first time since 1986 and avenge a 28-12 defeat at the hands of the Airlie Birds at this stage of last season's competition

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson assists a couple who have a £500,000 budget and a dream of leaving their city life in Bristol behind them and finding their perfect country home in Monmouthshire. While in the county, keen archaeologist Jules visits the village of Trellech, where local excavation efforts are unearthing what could be the largest medieval settlement in Wales.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are in Stoke-on-Trent to learn about an ambitious scheme to bring the countryside into the heart of the city. Margherita finds out what it takes to move a stretch of the River Trent, while Matt meets the next generation of nature lovers as he glories in mud with some of the city's youngest residents and their parents. In the light of the coronavirus pandemic, Tom Heap investigates whether the UK is sufficiently armed against further threats of diseases that spread from animals to humans, and Adam Henson meets another set of regional competitors vying to be crowned national One Man and His Dog champion

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain and the Blitz. Combining stories from all over the country with a special event filmed at Biggin Hill Airfield, this episode tells the story of a defining moment in Britain's war, through the people and objects who were there. Fiona begins on board the iconic Supermarine Spitfire, flying over the Weald of Kent. Then, she and the team uncover the stories from family mementos, including a scramble bell that got the pilots into the air, ops room models that tracked their every move and a steering column from a British fighter shot down in combat

programme.title: Extinction: The Facts
programme.desc: With a million species at threat, David Attenborough explores the science of extinction and how this has consequences for everyone. The programme examines why biodiversity loss is happening and what it means - not just for the natural world, but for all mankind. The loss of biodiversity threatens food and water security, undermines humans' ability to control the climate, and places humans at greater risk of pandemics. Leading experts including Sir Robert Watson and plant scientist Prof Kathy Willis explain how all groups in the natural world are in decline, and that extinction is now happening up to 100 times faster than the natural evolutionary rate

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Robin meets Raphael for a drink, and he explains more about Freddie's bullying of Rhiannon, which gives Geraint a strong motive for Chiswell's murder. They are interrupted by a call from Matthew, which Robin blocks before revealing that they have separated. The police show Robin and Strike CCTV footage of Kinvara at Paddington Station the night before Chiswell died, while Tegan reveals that she previously witnessed Kinvara hit Chiswell over the head with a hammer. Crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling that chronicle cases investigated by private detective Cormoran Strike. Starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Micah Richards to review the latest Premier League fixtures on the opening weekend of the season, featuring West Bromwich Albion v Leicester City at the Hawthorns and Tottenham Hotspur v Everton at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and newly promoted Manchester United

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: After losing their opening two games of the season, West Ham United's women's team are preparing to take on their bogey team Manchester City, who are packed full of England superstars. Within their own team, club captain Gilly Flaherty starts thinking about what life might be like when her football career comes to an end, French superstar Kenza Dali opens up about her mother's illness, and there is a rare appearance on the bench for academy player Victoria - who is still working hard on her GCSE exams. Narrated by Jaime Winstone

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits Wales to take a behind-the-scenes look at the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, revealing how its crew members keep people safe on the seas. Plus, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a fairly modern two-bedroom flat in Manchester, there is a revisit to a property in Bexleyheath, Kent, that was first seen in 2012, and Dion Dublin is in Wolverhampton examining a semi-detached house

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Cameras follow drug raids across Lancashire and beyond. Plus, an impressive racing car is put up for sale at the proceeds-of-crime auction and the tale of how a woman helped police get the better of a romance fraudster in Durham is recounted. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates the scandal of a north London bedsit that's simply not fit for purpose. He also joins the Birmingham empty homes officer who's fighting back against the city's 10,000 empty properties

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool Antique Centre. Host Christina Trevanion finds out about naturalist and painter John James Audubon's Birds of America books, which are on display in the city's Central Library

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Nancy is shocked when Ellen reveals Lawrence's dark secret. Annie is forced to choose between her duty to Harry and her love for Adam, Bea discovers Finch has doubts about her motives for marrying Billy, and Tucker's murderous revenge has devastating consequences for all four of the women. Drama, starring Summer Strallen and Nathaniel Parker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explain how necessity often forced cooks to make the most of limited ingredients to create tasty dishes. They reveal that many popular meals were invented during hard times, from mince and dumplings to Scotch broths

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Cornwall on a property search with a couple who have £750,000 to realise their dream of starting their married life together near the Cornish coast. While in the area, Nicki visits Looe Harbour to learn about the importance of sustainable fishing methods, which is championed by the local fishmongers she meets

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Joanna, Robert and their son Morgan have just moved into their brand-new house in Wrexham. On finding the prospect of sorting out the bare garden quite daunting they call upon Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich to help clear the builder's rubble and provide some ideas. The family has a £6,500 budget to create a garden from scratch that delivers a real 'wow' factor, incorporating dramatic planting, big architectural features and a swanky hot tub area

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sisters Linda Atkinson and Pauline Murphy from Merseyside arrive with their mother's pride and joy - a chair created for the investiture ceremony of the Prince of Wales in 1969. One of 4,000 made and later auctioned off to the general public, it is now in need of urgent attention, spurring restorer Will Kirk into action. Also being given a much-needed makeover are an air-powered toy car and a RNLI donation box. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: The ex-footballer reflects on his upbringing in Belfast's Shankill district and his career at the top of his sport during the 1980s. Raised in Belfast's Shankill district, Norman went on to become the youngest player to score for Manchester United's first team, the youngest FA Cup scorer in history and the youngest ever footballer to play in World Cup finals. However, within a decade, his career was cut cruelly short. Speaking to Stephen Watson, Norman reveals the true story behind his sporting life - what it was like to have and then lose it all - and how he managed to rebuild his life. Featuring unseen archive material

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic gold medallists in hockey and cycling respectively Sam Quek and Elinor Barker, former England goalkeeper Robert Green and Great Britain sprinter Asha Philip join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The Browns await the fruits of their fraud after faking Grandad's death to get a payout, but the insurance company starts to grow suspicious. Agnes struggles to keep him out of sight, as she has promised the money to Dermot and Maria, who are desperate to get a place of their own

programme.title: Zodiac (2007)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime thriller about the tortuous hunt for a mysterious serial killer who terrorised San Francisco in the late 1960s and early 1970s, and taunted the police and media with cryptic messages. Three men became obsessed with identifying the murderer after his reign of terror inexplicably stopped, but their fixation with the case took a heavy toll on their lives. Starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Robert Downey Jr, Mark Ruffalo and Brian Cox

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Paul Ainsworth, along with special guest Charlotte Ritchie. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the first episode, Nadiya kicks things off with a series of her favourite recipes for classic bakes with a twist, including a blueberry scone pizza, and a spicy Asian take on a traditional toad-in-the-hole

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Alex Scott and Tim Cahill look ahead to the opening round of Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Leeds United and Tottenham Hotspur v Everton

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two Grade II-listed cottages in Bassaleg, south Wales and Canterbury, Kent, and a mid-terrace house in Wallasey, Merseyside

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe guide two teams scouring Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire for bargains to take to auction. The reds show David how to be cool, while the blues go in search of a duck-billed platypus. Plus, presenter Eric Knowles pays a visit to the Museum of Timekeeping in nearby Upton to find out about a local man's mission to save the St Pancras Railway Clock

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A newly engaged couple are joined by Jules Hudson on a house-hunting mission in south Worcestershire, looking to purchase their first joint home with a budget of £300,000

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne scours the skips at Woking Recycling Centre. A dull dresser is given some makeover magic by Sarah Peterson, and Bruce Faulseit transforms a pair of lounge doors

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland. With Dion Dublin and Tim Cahill

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a sensory garden for two autistic girls in Aylesbury, while also providing a garden in Liphook for Anne, Shaun and daughter Joanne

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a mortgage advisor, a mother and daughter, and a print manager

programme.desc: As autumn draws to an end, Sweeny makes a deal with the local shop to sell his meat and donates some of his chickens to a college, where they will be looked after by the students

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Jane Harley performs tests on European bison at Highland Wildlife Park, and George Peplinski carries out delicate surgery on a dog that involves creating an eyelid from its lip

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Sarah Jane learns the truth about Alasdair after she's delivered her news to a devastated Cailean

programme.title: Curaidh na Coille/Birdman of Pollok

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature, alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Tom, Geoff, Bim and Alex work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Belle voices her concerns that by rushing the divorce Andrea will react badly, and matters become more complicated when Andrea returns home and learns that Belle has already moved in. Dawn gives her version of events to Will, but conveniently edits Harriet's out of the story, while Tracy does her worst putting the kibosh on Amy's hopes of dating Matty

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David discovers from Nina that Shona is planning to leave because she thinks he hates her, while a heartbroken Tim is worried for Elaine's safety and resolves to track her down. As Nick, Leanne and Steve make preparations for their holiday in Cornwall, they're interrupted by the sound of Oliver's seizure alarm, meaning a trip to the hospital

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the last of this daily run of the big-prize quiz, inviting another line-up of contestants to answer the questions that could change their lives - with the lifelines Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams presents a further compilation of TV clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. Clips include Olly Murs being attacked by a fly on The Voice, a goat running amok in Judge Rinder's court, a Love islander discovering the danger of wearing high heels on TV, and a desperate dog relieving himself on Paul O'Grady

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Vinnie Jones joins Piers Morgan for an intimate Life Stories special, 10 years on from his first appearance on the show. Vinnie opens up about how his life has changed irrevocably in that time, as he comes to terms with grieving for the loss of his wife, Tanya, who passed away last year, after a six-year battle with cancer. He also looks back at some of the highlights of his football and movie careers, talks about his struggles with alcohol, how he is hoping to help other people, particularly men, to cope with grief and what the future now holds

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mighty Mike

programme.title: Buck & Buddy

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Nya, Jay, Cole and Kai face off against the Ice Emperor's dreaded dragon, while Lloyd and his newfound allies, Kataru and Grimfax, confront the Ice Emperor himself.

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
programme.desc: When Thanos, Ronan, Nebula and the Ravagers seek to possess powerful and creative relic, the fate of the universe depends on the Guardians of the Galaxy to protect it

programme.title: Marvel's Spider-Man
programme.desc: The hero sets out to stop the evil Green Goblin and Venom from stealing key pieces of technology that could ultimately destroy New York City

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by singers Roachford and Beverley Knight, and chefs Lenny Carr-Roberts and Lesley Waters. James prepares red mullet, mussels and salmon, presides over a masterclass in batter, and takes a culinary trip to Edinburgh

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott recreates some of his mother's recipes, including brown chicken stew and rice n' peas. Country music duo the Shires take over the kitchen for a jam session and a late-night pasta feast, while Juliet Sear shares her mother's secret quiche recipe and Ainsley knocks up some homemade garlic bread and coconut and lime fairy cakes

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The next leg of James's trip sees him heading to Bresse, home of one of the country's most famous ingredients - the Bresse chicken. He visits a poultry farm to discover just why these birds are so special, and gets the chance to cook his own. Moving on, he meets Georges Blanc, owner of a three-Michelin-starred restaurant - and one of James's culinary heroes

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Coverage of this afternoon's meeting from Doncaster, which includes the fifth and final classic of the season, the 3.35 St Leger. Supporting races include the 1.50 Champagne Stakes, 2.25 Portland Handicap and the 3.00 Park Stakes. Plus, the 3.10 Matron Stakes and 4.10 Irish Champion Stakes on the first day of Irish Champions Weekend at Leopardstown, and two races from Chester. Presented by Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani, with analysis from Jason Weaver and Kevin Blake, reports from Matt Chapman, Sally Ann Grassick, Luke Harvey and Oli Bell, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with Larry Lamb, Sam Quek and Scarlett Moffatt all trying to guess the familiar phrases in the animated clues. If they can say what they see, they could get the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity.

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Giles Coren, Rachel Johnson, Chris Packham and Josie Long pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the second semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: Jonathan Ross' Comedy Club
programme.desc: The host presents a stand-up comedy showcase, featuring sets by some of the most exciting new stand-ups in the UK. Mawaan Rizwan co-hosts the first episode, which features Kae Kurd, Flo & Joan and Sophie Duker, as well as some new material from Rob Beckett

programme.title: Hacksaw Ridge (2016)
programme.desc: During the Second World War, US Army Medic Desmond Doss refuses to kill people, and becomes the first man in American history to receive the Medal of Honour without firing a shot. Andrew Garfield, Sam Worthington and Luke Bracey star in this biographical war film directed by Mel Gibson and written by Andrew Knight and Robert Schenkkan, based on the 2004 documentary The Conscientious Objector

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 14 of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a flat 194km course from Clermont-Ferrand to Lyon. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Small Fortune
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Dermot O'Leary, in which teams of three take on challenges in miniature settings. A team of dancers from Glasgow who appeared on the previous week's episode return to complete their challenge, while a trio of car dealers also compete for the cash prize of up to £150,000

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tries for his AeroFizz pilot's license. Magoo ruins everything when, convinced he's fixing his roof, he pierces a hole in the airship.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel shoot people with toy arrows to make them fall in love with Fizz. But Magoo mistakes them for a giant hornet.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz rehearses a dance number that'll make him a star. But when Weasel catapults him next door onto the roof, Magoo thinks he's a stray alien

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Animated reboot of the classic children's series. Virgil attempts to rescue a mining crew trapped inside a glacier, but the scientist in charge of the project refuses to leave.

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny goes extreme window cleaning, but will he be brave enough to abseil down the building and avoid the forfeit?

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz, controlling a robot, distributes ice cream to everyone in the city. When Magoo mistakes him for a monster, he gets sprayed with ice cream.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is at the foot of a volcano, but in fact, he's next to a huge soup vat. And he sets it all awry.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz invents a cucumber that turns people into an object of worship. When Magoo sees people being drawn to Fizz and Weasel, he thinks it's zombies!

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Stephan brings a robotic dog home, and Mike decides to use it as a stunt double so he can impress Iris.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: On a very hot day, Mike invites Iris to join him for a cooling cocktail on the terrace, but disaster strikes when the raccoons steal the ice maker.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Billy Ocean, Frankie Bridge and Russell Howard join Martin and Roman Kemp for the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Wildlife presenter Ben Fogle and actress Phyllis Logan join the presenter in his celebration of the great British countryside. Alan also meets a master willow-basket weaver, looks back to the flower trends of the 1980s and samples British sparkling wine with Clodagh McKenna. Plus, more from the organic halal farming family the Radwans

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford visit the country's most fascinating attractions. This week they are at the Black Country Living Museum in the West Midlands, where they drive vintage vehicles and learn the traditional skills of canal boat art and chain-making

programme.title: Love Your Garden Themed Specials
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh looks back on memorable family gardens created in the series, celebrating the unusual twists the team have put on creating the perfect environment for children. The programme focuses on three episodes in particular, in which the experts created a hangout for teenagers, a woodland adventure garden, and a fairy-tale glade

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott recreates some of his mother's recipes, including brown chicken stew and rice n' peas. Country music duo the Shires take over the kitchen for a jam session and a late night pasta feast, while Juliet Sear shares her mother's secret quiche recipe and Ainsley knocks up some homemade garlic bread and coconut and lime fairy cakes

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the second semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Ciro, Paiton, Simon and Anne work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature, alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as comedian Paul Merton, actress Sally Lindsay and Made in Chelsea's Jamie Laing take part in the celebrity version of the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Captain Sir Tom Moore joins Piers Morgan to look back on his extraordinary life - which spans an entire century. He reflects on the devastating accident that left him in intensive care at the age of 98 - and how his gratitude to the NHS and battle to regain mobility led to the record-breaking charity walk which first brought him to public attention. Tom takes stock of his wartime service, reveals the untold story of his first marriage and discusses how his beloved second wife Pamela brought him happiness and a family before her terminal illness left him rebuilding his life once again

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: For rubber baron Walter Blackett and his family, life in 1941 Singapore is full of wealth and privilege, where the horrors of war-torn Europe are a distant whisper. But when their power and stability are rocked by new arrivals and the looming threat of a Japanese invasion, Walter has a cunning plan. Adaptation of the novel by Booker Prize-winner JG Farrell, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Charles Dance, Jane Horrocks and Colm Meaney

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams presents a further compilation of TV clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. Clips include Olly Murs being attacked by a fly on The Voice, a goat running amok in Judge Rinder's court, a Love islander discovering the danger of wearing high heels on TV, and a desperate dog relieving himself on Paul O'Grady

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Staff at the airport deal with one of the UK's worst winter storms, with winds raging out on the airfield. Meanwhile, rookie passenger experience manager Lee finds himself dealing with his first emergency fire

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 15, featuring a mountainous 174.5km course from Lyon to Grand Colombier. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is at the foot of a volcano, but in fact, he's next to a huge soup vat. And he sets it all awry.

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Stephan brings a robotic dog home, and Mike decides to use it as a stunt double so he can impress Iris.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: On a very hot day, Mike invites Iris to join him for a cooling cocktail on the terrace, but disaster strikes when the raccoons steal the ice maker.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Billy Ocean, Frankie Bridge and Russell Howard join Martin and Roman Kemp for the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Wildlife presenter Ben Fogle and actress Phyllis Logan join the presenter in his celebration of the great British countryside. Alan also meets a master willow-basket weaver, looks back to the flower trends of the 1980s and samples British sparkling wine with Clodagh McKenna. Plus, more from the organic halal farming family the Radwans

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford visit the country's most fascinating attractions. This week they are at the Black Country Living Museum in the West Midlands, where they drive vintage vehicles and learn the traditional skills of canal boat art and chain-making

programme.title: Love Your Garden Themed Specials
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh looks back on memorable family gardens created in the series, celebrating the unusual twists the team have put on creating the perfect environment for children. The programme focuses on three episodes in particular, in which the experts created a hangout for teenagers, a woodland adventure garden, and a fairy-tale glade

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott recreates some of his mother's recipes, including brown chicken stew and rice n' peas. Country music duo the Shires take over the kitchen for a jam session and a late night pasta feast, while Juliet Sear shares her mother's secret quiche recipe and Ainsley knocks up some homemade garlic bread and coconut and lime fairy cakes

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the second semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Ciro, Paiton, Simon and Anne work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature, alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as comedian Paul Merton, actress Sally Lindsay and Made in Chelsea's Jamie Laing take part in the celebrity version of the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Captain Sir Tom Moore joins Piers Morgan to look back on his extraordinary life - which spans an entire century. He reflects on the devastating accident that left him in intensive care at the age of 98 - and how his gratitude to the NHS and battle to regain mobility led to the record-breaking charity walk which first brought him to public attention. Tom takes stock of his wartime service, reveals the untold story of his first marriage and discusses how his beloved second wife Pamela brought him happiness and a family before her terminal illness left him rebuilding his life once again

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: For rubber baron Walter Blackett and his family, life in 1941 Singapore is full of wealth and privilege, where the horrors of war-torn Europe are a distant whisper. But when their power and stability are rocked by new arrivals and the looming threat of a Japanese invasion, Walter has a cunning plan. Adaptation of the novel by Booker Prize-winner JG Farrell, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Charles Dance, Jane Horrocks and Colm Meaney

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams presents a further compilation of TV clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. Clips include Olly Murs being attacked by a fly on The Voice, a goat running amok in Judge Rinder's court, a Love islander discovering the danger of wearing high heels on TV, and a desperate dog relieving himself on Paul O'Grady

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Staff at the airport deal with one of the UK's worst winter storms, with winds raging out on the airfield. Meanwhile, rookie passenger experience manager Lee finds himself dealing with his first emergency fire

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 15, featuring a mountainous 174.5km course from Lyon to Grand Colombier. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff shows Sally his true colours as he tries to assure Tim that he is the innocent party, while Dev shows an estate agent round the kebab shop, much to Asha and Aadi's concern. Shona spends the day with David in the salon and Daniel confides in Adam how he's worried about Nicky

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally and Faye try to download the deleted camera app and CCTV footage, while the police tell Geoff that new evidence has come to light and they need to ask him some more questions. David confides in Sarah that he worries Shona will never return to normal, and Nina calls to see Asha and gently suggests that she can do much better than Corey

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Businessman and owner of the Causton Lions rugby team Mark Adler is mysteriously found dead in the club's cryotherapy chamber, which is normally used by the players for physiotherapy. Barnaby and Winter are called in to investigate and their list of suspects soon includes Adler's wife Samantha, former coach Guy Bevan, troubled star player Danny Wickham and physio Neville Gallagher. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Tamzin Outhwaite, Don Gilet and Michael Maloney

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Barnaby is dragged along to the wedding of the son of Sarah's friend Hazel, the last thing he expects to find himself doing is investigating a murder. But when his wife discovers the body of the bride, Laurel, who has been squeezed to death by her own wedding dress corset, the detective must look more closely at his fellow guests. The victim's husband Gavin is convinced that his wife's death had everything to do with her outspoken nature on her radio show, so Winter's attention soon turns to her unique community of nocturnal listeners. Gavin's scorned ex-fiancee and local wedding dress designer Serena also comes under suspicion, and Barnaby and Winter's enquiries lead them to a local wedding fayre. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Kelly Brook, Nick Hancock and Liz Fraser

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Things take a gruesome turn when Ferabbees Circus comes to town, bringing with it a chain of sinister clown sightings, threatening notes and deathly dangerous circus acts. Barnaby has to face up to his fears in order to solve the case. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix, Lorraine Ashbourne and Annette Badland

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Will and Ellie go on their first date with a trip to the flicks. However, a scream from the projection booth cuts the romance short as they discover a brutal murder has taken place within the cinema. Geordie arrives to investigate and soon they've found movie star connections, oddball employees and a link to the Asian subcontinent, as a shadowy figure emerges from Jack Chapman's past and refuses to retreat without causing a stir. Cathy's mother takes the children out for the day, which ends up worrying Cathy far more than it should. Robson Green, Tom Brittney, Lauren Carse and Kacey Ainsworth star

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a young man is found dead and stark naked out on the Fens, Will and Geordie discover ties to a Cambridge research project experimenting with mind-altering drugs. Its arrogant professor is a proponent of the drug LSD in his psychotherapy sessions, but Will and Geordie start wondering whether there is more going on. As the case proceeds, Will's feelings of guilt about his father's death and his conflicted feelings about Ellie start to take their toll. Elsewhere, the Keatings suffer an awkward dinner party and the Chapman feud shows no sign of relenting. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Two-part drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and based on the novel by Peter Robinson. DCI Alan Banks investigates the most difficult case of his life when five girls go missing but only four bodies are found. Chief suspect Marcus Payne is in a coma, and ambitious DS Annie Cabbot from the Professional Standards department pursues Banks in an effort to find out why. Andrea Lowe and Charlotte Riley also star

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Conclusion. The mysterious death of serial killer Marcus Payne makes the task of finding his accomplice more difficult and holds up the search for Leanne Wray. With time against him and DS Annie Cabbot breathing down his neck, Banks follows a hunch and takes Payne's wife in for questioning on suspicion of involvement with the rape and murder of the four girls. The lead she provides is more startling than anything the detectives are prepared for. Drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Charlotte Riley and Andrea Lowe

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid permits Mike's dancer girlfriend to stay for a couple of days, but soon changes his mind when he finds out what sort of performer she really is. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dismisses Jean's interest in the daily horoscopes as complete nonsense - but his opinion changes when they start coming true

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff shows Sally his true colours as he tries to assure Tim that he is the innocent party, while Dev shows an estate agent round the kebab shop, much to Asha and Aadi's concern. Shona spends the day with David in the salon and Daniel confides in Adam how he's worried about Nicky
